    Welcome to ’s website

    received his Ph.D. from the Department of Chemical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Bombay (Mumbai, India) in Feb 2024, where he was a humble part of the Automation Lab. Just saying Ph.D. without mentioning the topic doesn’t make much sense. So, here goes: Sensor Network design for Chemical Processes broadly summarized his research work at IIT Bombay.

    He made this website out of curiosity. You are most welcome to give any constructive feedback (comments) on his posts. Also, you can check out the Automation Lab guide-student network created in D3.js. You will have fun playing with the graph.

    OP likes coding, trekking, running, engaging in several sports activities, capturing photos through his lenses (visit his photography page), learning about one or two new things, listening to Bollywood melodies, and watching action movies on his old laptop, which he borrowed from his sister a long time back. Well, he is not an expert in whatever he does, but he is keen to learn. Last but not least, I am still wondering why I wrote this para in 3rd person.

    Thank you for visiting the website.
